verificationNote: Requirements constructed from CSA CCM v4, NIST CSF 2.0, and Malaysia Act 854 obligations. Verify against authoritative sources before use in formal assessments.

domain.name: Identity and Access Management
domain.description: Requirements for managing who can access cloud resources, how they authenticate, and what permissions they hold. Cloud IAM must enforce least privilege, eliminate long-lived credentials, and provide full auditability of all access.

requirements.title: Multi-Factor Authentication for All Human Access
requirements.description: Enforce multi-factor authentication for all human users accessing cloud management consoles, APIs, and federated applications. Phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2, WebAuthn) required for privileged accounts. Root/owner accounts must use hardware security keys.

cspGuidance.aws: Enable MFA on all IAM users. Use SCPs to deny actions without MFA (aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent). Assign hardware MFA to root account. Configure IAM Identity Center with MFA requirement.
cspGuidance.azure: Configure Entra ID Conditional Access requiring MFA for all users. Use Authentication Strengths to enforce phishing-resistant MFA for admins. Enable Security Defaults as baseline.
cspGuidance.gcp: Enforce 2-Step Verification in Cloud Identity admin console. Require Titan Security Keys for super admin accounts. Use BeyondCorp Enterprise for context-aware access decisions.

requirements.title: Least Privilege Access and Permission Right-Sizing
requirements.description: Grant only the minimum permissions required for each cloud identity to perform its function. Avoid wildcard (*) actions in IAM policies. Use custom roles where built-in roles are too broad. Conduct quarterly access reviews to remove unused permissions.

cspGuidance.aws: Use IAM Access Analyzer to identify unused permissions. Create scoped IAM policies with specific resource ARNs. Enable Access Advisor for last-accessed analysis. Deny wildcard permissions via SCP.
cspGuidance.azure: Use Entra ID PIM for just-in-time role activation. Define custom RBAC roles scoped to resource groups. Use Access Reviews for quarterly recertification of privileged roles.
cspGuidance.gcp: Use IAM Recommender to right-size permissions. Create custom roles. Apply IAM Conditions for fine-grained access. Enable Policy Analyzer to identify over-privileged identities.

requirements.title: Service Account and Non-Human Identity Management
requirements.description: Manage service accounts, managed identities, and workload identities with the same rigour as human accounts. Eliminate long-lived credentials in favour of cloud-native workload identity federation and short-lived tokens. Inventory and review all non-human identities quarterly.

cspGuidance.aws: Use IAM Roles (instance profiles, task roles, execution roles) instead of access keys. Enable OIDC federation for external workloads. Disable and delete unused access keys. Rotate keys that must exist.
cspGuidance.azure: Use Managed Identities (system-assigned preferred) for all Azure services. Use Workload Identity Federation for external workloads. Set credential expiry on app registrations.
cspGuidance.gcp: Use attached service accounts instead of key files. Enable Workload Identity Federation for GKE and external workloads. Disable unused service account keys. Use IAM Conditions to restrict impersonation.

requirements.title: Identity Federation and Single Sign-On
requirements.description: Federate all cloud access through the enterprise identity provider using SAML 2.0 or OIDC. Eliminate local cloud accounts except for break-glass emergency access. Centralised authentication enables consistent MFA, conditional access, and session policies.

cspGuidance.aws: Configure AWS IAM Identity Center with external IdP. Map IdP groups to AWS permission sets. Enforce session duration limits. Disable direct IAM user console access where possible.
cspGuidance.azure: Entra ID as native IdP. Configure SAML/OIDC for third-party cloud accounts. Enable Conditional Access for session controls. Disable guest account creation for non-approved domains.
cspGuidance.gcp: Configure Cloud Identity federation with external IdP. Use Workforce Identity Federation for console and API. Map IdP groups to GCP IAM roles via group-based access.

requirements.title: Privileged Access Management
requirements.description: Manage privileged cloud access through just-in-time elevation, time-bound sessions, and approval workflows. Privileged roles include cloud administrators, security administrators, and any role with ability to modify IAM policies, encryption keys, or network configurations.

cspGuidance.aws: Use AWS IAM Identity Center with permission sets requiring approval. Deploy temporary elevated access via STS AssumeRole with session tags. Monitor privileged actions via CloudTrail.
cspGuidance.azure: Deploy Entra ID PIM for all privileged Azure AD and Azure RBAC roles. Configure approval workflows and time-bound activation. Enable access reviews for privileged roles.
cspGuidance.gcp: Use PAM (Privileged Access Manager) for just-in-time access. Configure IAM Conditions with time-bound access. Enable Data Access audit logs for privileged operations.
